These are appropriate.  \n- **Justification:** No signs of malicious activity.  \n\n**Report 4:**  \n- **Issue presence:** Similar to previous; standard export with base64 source map.  \n- **Errors/flaws:** The report notes a slight security risk (0.1) due to embedding source map data, but this is common and not inherently dangerous.  \n- **Scores:** Malware=0, Obfuscated=0, Risk=0.1.  \n- **Justification:** The small risk score is acceptable; embedding source maps can sometimes be misused, but in this context, it’s standard.  \n\n**Report 5:**  \n- **Issue presence:** Same pattern—generated index with embedded source map.  \n- **Errors/flaws:** No issues; reasoning is consistent.  \n- **Scores:** Malware=0, Obfuscated=0, Risk=0.  \n- **Justification:** No malicious intent detected.  \n\n---\n\n### Summary of findings:\n- **Code presence:** All reports correctly identify that the code is a standard, auto-generated module index with embedded source maps.  \n- **Malware:** All reports assign malware=0, which is justified.  \n- **Obfuscation:** All reports correctly identify no obfuscation.  \n- **Risk scores:** Nearly all are 0, with one at 0.1, which is reasonable given the embedded source map data.  \n\n### Final assessment:\n- **Malware score:** 0 across all reports; no malicious activity detected.  \n- **Obfuscated score:** 0; code is straightforward and generated.  \n- **Risk score:** Should remain very low, around 0 to 0.1, reflecting the minor concern about embedded source maps, but this is standard practice.  \n\n### My overall estimate:  \n- **Malware:** 0  \n- **Obfuscated:** 0  \n- **Risk:** 0.05 (to reflect minimal concern about embedded source maps, but overall very safe)  \n\n**Conclusion:**  \nThe reports are accurate and consistent.
